question: How should citations be audited in deep research agents?

claim_lines: Audit citations in deep research agents at the **claim level**: split the report into atomic statements, map each statement to the exact supporting source span(s), and score both **citation precision** and **citation recall**. Treat citations as evidence, not decoration, and flag any claim that is unsupported or only loosely supported. ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.04499))
- **claim-level evidence**: Use atomic claim decomposition and claim-to-source support matrices rather than only report-level scoring. ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.04499))

- **source relevance**: Check that each citation is relevant to the claim and to the intended source set; current deep-research benchmarks score citation accuracy and citation association. ([deepresearch-bench.github.io](https://deepresearch-bench.github.io/?utm_source=openai))
- **citation granularity**: Require tight, sentence-level support so each cite supports its associated statement. ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.09848))
- **unsupported claim detection**: Flag statements that are not supported by their cited sources; this failure mode is common in audited systems. ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.04499))

- **handling conflicting sources**: If sources disagree, keep both citations visible, mark the claim uncertain, and resolve only by explicit review; this is a recommendation inferred from source-grounded evaluation guidance. ([help.openai.com](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/10500283-deep-research-faq?_bhlid=0b076fa13fbdaacdbc75dca9b2aee1d3e2de19a7))

Direct source-backed facts: deep research outputs are designed to include citations or source links for verification, the API can emit inline citations plus source metadata, and primary evaluations define verifiability in terms of citation precision/recall and statement-level support. ([help.openai.com](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/10500283-deep-research-faq?_bhlid=0b076fa13fbdaacdbc75dca9b2aee1d3e2de19a7))

Recommended audit workflow: (1) decompose the report into atomic claims; (2) map each claim to source spans; (3) score support, relevance, and completeness; (4) flag unsupported or weakly supported claims; (5) if sources conflict, label the claim contested instead of silently collapsing the disagreement. ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.04499))
